~~~
<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.Begin -->
<!--
    # This information is used for caching.
    [PlutoStaticHTML.State]
    input_sha = "1d3832c5a95c2253c7cf1deef42b49144e42633e0ee283da392b62607ff4ada8"
    julia_version = "1.12.4"
-->




~~~
+++
title = "An ordinary differential equation"
+++

~~~


<div class="markdown"><h1 id="An-ordinary-differential-equation">An ordinary differential equation</h1><p>This page shows a part of a tutorial by <a href="https://github.com/SciML/">SciML</a> (<a href="https://docs.sciml.ai/DiffEqDocs/stable/examples/classical_physics/">https://docs.sciml.ai/DiffEqDocs/stable/examples/classical_physics/</a>).</p><p>In the tutorial, a simple harmonic oscillator is shown. It is given by</p><p class="tex">$$\ddot{x} + \omega^2 x = 0$$</p><p>with the analytical solution</p><p class="tex">$$\begin{eqnarray}
x(t) &amp;= A \cos (\omega t - \phi) \\
v(t) &amp;= - A \omega \sin (\omega t - \phi)
\end{eqnarray}$$</p><p>where</p><p class="tex">$$A = \sqrt{c_1 + c_2} \: \: \: \: \: \text{and} \: \: \: \: \: \tan \phi = \frac{c_2}{c_1}$$</p><p>with <span class="tex">\(c_1\)</span>, <span class="tex">\(c_2\)</span> constants determined by the initial conditions such that <span class="tex">\(c_1\)</span> is the initial position and <span class="tex">\(\omega c_2\)</span> is the initial velocity.</p><p>Instead of transforming this to a system of ODEs to solve with <code>ODEProblem</code>, we can use <code>SecondOrderODEProblem</code> as follows.</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>using OrdinaryDiffEq, Plots</code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><p>With parameter:</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>ω = 1;</code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><p>and initial conditions:</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>x₀ = [0.0];</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>dx₀ = [π / 2];</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>tspan = (0.0, 2π);</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>ϕ = atan((dx₀[1] / ω) / x₀[1]);</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>A = √(x₀[1]^2 + dx₀[1]^2);</code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><p>We define the problem as follows:</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>function harmonicoscillator(ddu, du, u, ω, t)
    ddu .= -ω^2 * u
end;</code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><p>And pass it to the solvers:</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>prob = SecondOrderODEProblem(harmonicoscillator, dx₀, x₀, tspan, ω);</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>sol = solve(prob, DPRKN6())</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-sol">retcode: Success
Interpolation: specialized 6th order interpolation
t: 21-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.0
 0.0006362147454811361
 0.010180207933076954
 0.04804241764385478
 0.12816946395559364
 0.250842712371822
 0.4093033665532287
 ⋮
 3.6758814439731107
 4.1413392906488715
 4.641874215009247
 5.199947963397019
 5.823354512663183
 6.283185307179586
u: 21-element Vector{RecursiveArrayTools.ArrayPartition{Float64, Tuple{Vector{Float64}, Vector{Float64}}}}:
 ([1.5707963267948966], [0.0])
 ([1.570796008889919], [0.0009993637178359062])
 ([1.5707149314762023], [0.015990757019241676])
 ([1.5689839184362309], [0.0754358267723675])
 ([1.5579119205978385], [0.20077735911161132])
 ([1.5216360367069885], [0.38990367025826794])
 ([1.4410458560452288], [0.625130339363116])
 ⋮
 ([-1.3518757589309065], [-0.7998953985772836])
 ([-0.8490394018385742], [-1.3215646890993369])
 ([-0.11067221593113467], [-1.5668928568020466])
 ([0.7358722626762072], [-1.3877655377446527])
 ([1.4076336577155995], [-0.6971128331231436])
 ([1.570795569343618], [9.764294399629317e-7])</pre>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>let
    plot(
        sol,
        vars=[2, 1],
        linewidth=2,
        title="Simple Harmonic Oscillator",
        xaxis="Time",
        yaxis="Elongation",
        label=["x" "dx"],
    )
    plot!(t -&gt; A * cos(ω * t - ϕ), lw=3, ls=:dash, label="Analytical Solution x")
    plot!(t -&gt; -A * ω * sin(ω * t - ϕ), lw=3, ls=:dash, label="Analytical Solution dx")
end</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"/>
<div class='manifest-versions'>
<p>Built with Julia 1.12.4 and</p>
OrdinaryDiffEq 6.108.0<br>
Plots 1.41.6
</div>

<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.End -->
~~~

_To run this tutorial locally, download [this file](/tutorials/ode.jl) and open it with
[Pluto.jl](https://plutojl.org)._
